commit.message:
refactor(repo): remove elixir implementation

Summary:
- remove the Elixir reference implementation from the repository
- rewrite the README for the current TypeScript-only service
- keep the documented setup focused on WORKFLOW.md, Node, and the
  current CLI and status surfaces

Rationale:
- the repository has been converted to the TypeScript implementation
- keeping the deleted Elixir tree and README references would be stale
- the updated docs now match the shipped runtime and validation model

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(workflow): add default repo workflow

Summary:
- add a root WORKFLOW.md for the TypeScript service default startup path
- use a non-Elixir prompt and runtime config with local workspace defaults
- add a repository-level test that requires the shipped workflow to validate

Rationale:
- the CLI defaults to ./WORKFLOW.md, so the repo should ship a valid file
- keeping only elixir/WORKFLOW.md made the default runtime path ambiguous
- the test prevents future regressions back to an unusable default startup

Tests:
- npm test -- test/repository-workflow.test.ts
- npm test
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(host): harden shutdown and template diagnostics

Summary:
- add signal-managed host shutdown flow and keep main running until
  the service is explicitly stopped
- return 405 for unsupported methods on known status routes and add
  integration smoke tests that skip cleanly unless enabled
- isolate logger sink write failures and split template parse errors
  from template render errors

Rationale:
- the spec expects host lifecycle to handle process signals cleanly
  rather than exiting immediately after startup
- API method semantics and clearer template diagnostics improve
  operator behavior and conformance with the repo contract
- log sink isolation prevents observability failures from breaking the
  orchestrator during normal runtime

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(runtime): add session tracking and resilient reload flow

Summary:
- forward Codex runtime events through the agent runner into the
  orchestrator and expose live session metadata in snapshots and
  status responses
- add stall timeout handling plus resilient startup, reconciliation,
  candidate-fetch, and workflow-reload behavior that keeps the last
  known good config active
- add CLI --port parsing and coverage for the new orchestrator and
  host lifecycle behavior

Rationale:
- the spec requires the orchestrator to be the authority for live run
  state, which needs session event ingestion rather than log-only
  visibility
- tracker and workflow failures should degrade dispatch safely instead
  of crashing ticks or startup paths
- CLI port selection is part of the optional HTTP extension contract
  and removes the need to encode all server behavior in WORKFLOW.md

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat!: migrate from chatgpt specific spec to offical mcp app spec (#23)

* feat(mcp-apps): migrate template to MCP Apps spec

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(deps): downgrade zod to v3 for MCP SDK compatibility

Resolves "v3Schema.safeParseAsync is not a function" error when calling tools. The MCP SDK claims to support Zod v4 but its code still uses v3 APIs like safeParseAsync() which were removed in v4.

Changes:
- Downgrade zod from ^4.2.1 to ^3.25.76 in server/package.json
- Add npm overrides in root package.json to force all dependencies to use Zod v3
- Regenerate package-lock.json with consistent Zod v3 across all packages

This is a temporary workaround until the MCP SDK is updated to properly support Zod v4.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(zod): align with MCP Apps sdk

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* feat(zod): upgrade to v4 and update EchoToolInputSchema for improved validation

* fix: got it working in VS Code

* wip

* feat(server): add UI capability negotiation and text-only fallback

Check client capabilities via getUiCapability to conditionally bind
UI resources to tools. Clients that don't advertise MCP Apps support
now receive text-only responses. Also fixes Zod error property access
(error.errors → error.issues).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(widgets): improve type safety and align with MCP Apps spec

- Add isEchoToolOutput type guard for safer structuredContent handling
- Fix requestDisplayMode return type to match spec ({ mode } object)
- Use TextContent type assertion for content array filtering
- Fix mock-app to return proper requestDisplayMode response
- Safely handle plugins array in Storybook viteFinal config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update architecture decision for McpServer + MCP Apps helpers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): remove non-standard CSP metadata fields

Remove scriptDomains and styleDomains from dev CSP config as they
are not part of the MCP Apps spec. Scripts, styles, fonts, and images
are all covered by resourceDomains per the McpUiResourceCsp spec.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(widgets): replace viewport with containerDimensions per MCP Apps spec

The spec defines containerDimensions (with height, maxHeight, width,
maxWidth) instead of the non-standard viewport.maxHeight field.
Updated HostContext type, Echo component, mock, and stories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(widgets): remove jest-dom and fix mock callback reference

- Remove @testing-library/jest-dom (unnecessary with vitest)
- Replace toBeInTheDocument() with toBeTruthy()
- Add path alias to vitest config for @/ imports
- Fix mock-app spread bug that broke ontoolresult callback
  (connect() referenced the original object while the component
  set callbacks on the spread copy)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(widgets): demo openLink, sendMessage, updateModelContext and improve UX

Add new MCP Apps capability demos to the Echo widget:
- Open Docs: calls openLink() to open an external URL via the host
- Send to Chat: calls sendMessage() to send a message to the host chat
- Update Context: calls updateModelContext() to push widget state to
  the model's context, with a dedicated output section showing the
  pretty-printed JSON payload

UI improvements:
- Add icons to all action buttons (Play, X, ExternalLink, etc.)
- Move fullscreen toggle to top-right as icon-only button with
  Maximize2/Minimize2 icons that swap based on display mode
- Add title tooltips to all buttons explaining which API they call
- Fix content jump by setting min-height on result output
- Merge partial host context updates instead of replacing to fix
  fullscreen toggle requiring two clicks
- Update local display mode state from requestDisplayMode response
- Reorder buttons: Call Echo Tool, Update Context, Clear, Send to
  Chat, Open Docs

Also updates AppLike type and mock with openLink, sendMessage,
and updateModelContext methods.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(widgets): improve echo widget padding, rounded corners, and stable height

Increase default padding to 16px with a minimum floor so fullscreen mode
doesn't lose padding, add rounded-2xl corners, and reserve space for the
model context update section so the widget height stays consistent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(widgets): always enable Send to Chat button

The button sent a hardcoded demo message, not the tool output, so
gating it behind toolOutput was unnecessary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(Echo): remove min-height from main container style

* docs: document MCP Apps features added in mcp-app-compat branch

Document display modes (inline/pip/fullscreen), App API runtime methods
(openLink, sendMessage, updateModelContext, requestDisplayMode),
UI capability negotiation, inline widget assets, container dimensions,
and the createMockApp() test helper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(widgets): remove duplicate isDev declaration in vite plugin

The variable was declared twice in the same scope, causing esbuild to
fail during `vite build --watch`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* wip

* fix(dependencies): update @modelcontextprotocol/ext-apps to version 1.2.2

* feat(server): use base64 data URIs for inline widget assets (Claude.ai compat)

Claude.ai doesn't allow serving widgets from localhost like ChatGPT and
VS Code do, requiring fully self-contained HTML via INLINE_WIDGET_ASSETS.
Its sandboxed iframe on claudemcpcontent.com also uses document.write()
to inject HTML, which breaks on raw inline JS containing backticks.
Switch to base64 data URIs for JS and inline <style> tags for CSS to
work within Claude.ai's stricter CSP. Adds pre-inlining cache at startup
and file watcher for dev rebuild support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: rename INLINE_WIDGET_ASSETS to CLAUDE_DEV_MODE

Reframe the inline widget assets env var as Claude.ai-specific local
dev mode since it's only used via `npm run dev:claude`. Document that
custom fonts won't load in Claude.ai due to its CSP restrictions
(font-src 'self' https://assets.claude.ai) and clarify this mode is
not needed in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add INLINE_DEV_MODE for remote sharing with inlined fonts

Add a second inline mode alongside CLAUDE_DEV_MODE:
- CLAUDE_DEV_MODE: inlines JS/CSS (fonts fall back to system stack due to CSP)
- INLINE_DEV_MODE: inlines JS/CSS + woff2 fonts as base64 data URIs

Use `npm run dev:inline` when sharing your MCP app remotely via tunnel
so viewers see custom fonts. Works with hosts like ChatGPT that don't
restrict font-src.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: document Pomerium requirement and widget hosting options

MCP server must be behind a Pomerium route for OAuth and access
policies. Widget assets must be publicly accessible via the same
server, a CDN, or a static host like Netlify/Vercel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: document CSP resourceDomains for loading external resources in widgets

MCP Apps hosts sandbox widgets in iframes with strict CSP that blocks
remote images by default. Documenting that resourceDomains must be
declared in _meta.ui.csp to allow external image/font/API domains.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove unused build-all script

* refactor: consolidate inline dev modes into single INLINE_DEV_MODE

Remove CLAUDE_DEV_MODE in favor of a single INLINE_DEV_MODE flag that
handles both Claude.ai and remote sharing. Fonts now load via Google
Fonts instead of inlined data URIs. Also removes redundant
assetsInlineLimit default and unused build-all.mts script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add base url to csp config

* fix: adjust resourceDomains based on INLINE_DEV_MODE

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: chatgpt-app-typescript-template
repository.description: ChatGPT app template using Pomerium, OpenAI Apps SDK and Model Context Protocol (MCP), with a Node.js server and React widgets.

commit.message:
feat(api): expand status and harden linear graphql

Summary:
- expand the status server with issue, list, health, ready, refresh,
  and reconcile endpoints plus normalized summary payloads
- harden the linear_graphql client tool with stricter input
  validation and structured transport, status, json, and graphql
  failure results
- add broader lifecycle logging across service, runner, status, and
  Codex app-server flows
- add focused tests for the new API surface, logging, and tool error
  handling

Rationale:
- operators need richer runtime inspection and control endpoints than
  a raw snapshot to debug active and retrying work
- linear graphql calls should fail predictably with structured errors
  so agent sessions can reason about tracker-side failures safely
- deeper logging coverage makes runtime transitions traceable without
  attaching a debugger to the daemon

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test: unify raw-io guardrail checks

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Replace the synthetic task-system AC fixture with a checked-in snapshot of the real backfilled @tasks refs and acceptance criteria.

Task: @01KKB8FF9NXTCSJNYZZKHNR4KD
Spec: @tasks-ac-backfill

feat(observability): add logging and live status integrations

Summary:
- add a structured logger and wire it into orchestrator and service
  lifecycle paths
- advertise and execute the linear_graphql dynamic tool in the Codex
  app-server client
- expand the status API with refresh and issue-scoped endpoints and
  rebind the status server when workflow config changes port
- add coverage for logging, service reload, status endpoints, and
  linear tool execution

Rationale:
- operators need stable runtime logs and richer status endpoints to
  understand scheduler behavior without attaching a debugger
- agent sessions need controlled access to raw Linear GraphQL calls so
  workflow-driven runs can use Symphony's tracker integration
- live port rebind keeps workflow reload behavior closer to the spec by
  applying server changes without a full process restart

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run build

commit.message:
feat: agent-first pivot — detection, approval gate, re-review loop, landing page

Add agent detection (bot accounts, PR body markers, branch prefixes, co-author
patterns), structured JSON review output in PR comments, dynamic APPROVE/
REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT gate, re-review loop with finding resolution tracking,
agent-tuned LLM prompts, status API endpoints, @codevetter mention trigger,
and redesigned landing page with animated graphics reflecting the agent-first
positioning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(runtime): add codex runner and orchestrator service

Summary:
- add a live Codex app-server stdio client with handshake, turn
  streaming, timeout handling, and high-trust approval/tool behavior
- implement the agent runner, long-running orchestrator loop, status
  server, service bootstrap, and CLI/main entrypoint wiring
- add runtime-focused tests covering the Codex client, agent runner,
  orchestrator behavior, status endpoints, and CLI startup paths

Rationale:
- connect the previously implemented workflow, tracker, workspace, and
  scheduling modules into a runnable Symphony service shape
- cover the remaining major runtime layers from the spec so the
  TypeScript implementation is no longer just a library foundation
- keep the new runtime surfaces exercised by focused tests before any
  future work on richer logging, dynamic tools, or broader API surface

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test: replace internal pause checks with event behavior

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: add symphony spec baseline and TypeScript core

Summary:
- add the initial Symphony repository content, including SPEC.md,
  repository skills, GitHub workflows, and the Elixir reference
  implementation
- scaffold a new TypeScript application at the repo root for a
  non-Elixir implementation of the Symphony specification
- implement and test workflow parsing, reload handling, workspace
  safety, Linear read integration, and core orchestration rules/state

Rationale:
- establish the upstream specification and reference materials in the
  repository so the new implementation has a documented source of truth
- start the non-Elixir implementation with test-driven, reusable core
  modules before adding the long-running runtime and app-server layers
- keep the current work focused on spec-conformant foundations that can
  support the remaining orchestrator, Codex client, and operator
  surfaces

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Migrate server tests to shared fixture helpers

commit.message:
fix(test): isolate schema AC backfill review fixture

Replace repo-root initContext(process.cwd()) usage in the schema backfill review tests with a self-contained temp spec fixture so shard 1 does not depend on ambient repository metadata.

Task: @01KKB8FE79G64FCFBRF9C4ADM7
Spec: @schema-ac-backfill
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): add startup mailbox timeout faults

Summary:
- add a mailbox-local `:drop_response` protocol fault so simulator
  devices can suppress specific CoE replies without faking transport
  loss
- add scenario 22 covering PREOP startup mailbox response timeouts for
  segmented downloads and document the new fault kind in the simulator
  docs
- update the simulator loop notes to move the next gap to public SDO
  helper timeout coverage

Rationale:
- startup mailbox failures should be able to model a slave that accepts
  a request but never produces the expected mailbox reply
- this keeps mailbox timeout behavior visible at the correct layer as a
  precise `:response_timeout`, instead of collapsing it into a generic
  bus timeout or malformed response path

Validation:
- mix test test/integration/simulator/22_startup_mailbox_response_timeout_test.exs test/integration/simulator
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): cover segmented startup mailbox faults

Summary:
- add malformed segmented download-ack coverage for public SDO flows
- add startup PREOP coverage for segmented mailbox ack faults
- harden the simulator integration harness against UDP bind reuse

Rationale:
- extend the simulator README loop into the next mailbox protocol edge
- keep startup restart scenarios deterministic instead of relying on socket timing

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/slave/mailbox/coe_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/20_malformed_download_segment_ack_sdo_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/21_startup_segmented_download_ack_fault_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(simulator): cover malformed segmented CoE responses

Summary:
- add simulator mailbox protocol faults for invalid segmented upload
  padding and unexpected segment commands
- tighten CoE segment parsing to report unexpected segment commands as a
  structured error instead of raw response blobs
- add parser-level tests and integration scenario 19 for malformed
  segmented upload responses, and move the README frontier to segmented
  download-side faults

Rationale:
- segmented mailbox regressions should surface as small parser errors the
  caller can act on, not opaque raw-frame tuples
- the simulator loop is clearer when upload-segment corruption is covered
  separately before extending into malformed download acknowledgements

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/slave/mailbox/coe_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/18_malformed_coe_payload_sdo_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/19_malformed_segment_response_sdo_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): add malformed CoE payload faults

Summary:
- add simulator mailbox protocol faults for invalid CoE payloads and
  unexpected SDO commands during upload-init handling
- add parser-level CoE coverage plus integration scenario 18 for
  malformed payloads that stay inside valid mailbox framing
- move the simulator loop docs forward to the next segmented CoE
  payload frontier

Rationale:
- mailbox regressions should surface as exact parser failures instead of
  looking like transport loss or generic mailbox faults
- the integration loop stays useful when each new parser-visible fault
  gets one focused scenario and the README advances with it

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/slave/mailbox/coe_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/17_malformed_mailbox_response_sdo_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/18_malformed_coe_payload_sdo_test.exs
- mix test test/integration/simulator
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): expand mailbox protocol fault coverage

Summary:
- add mailbox protocol faults for bad counters, toggle mismatches,
  wrong mailbox types, and wrong CoE services in the simulator mailbox
  runtime
- add integration scenarios 16 and 17 plus CoE parser coverage for exact
  mailbox protocol error tuples
- refresh simulator loop docs and raise the shared simulator ring test
  frame timeout to 20ms to avoid cold-start config flaps

Rationale:
- mailbox faults should stay visible as exact CoE/parser failures instead
  of looking like transport or topology loss
- the simulator integration loop is only useful if the standalone suite is
  stable enough to run repeatably, not just inside the full suite

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/slave/mailbox/coe_test.exs \

feat(simulator): add mailbox step milestones

Summary:
- add mailbox-step milestone tracking for successful CoE upload and
  download progress in the simulator runtime
- add a larger mailbox profile object and scenario 15 to prove delayed
  segmented aborts after real mailbox progress
- update simulator docs and scenario notes to move the next work item
  to mailbox protocol-shape faults

Rationale:
- segmented mailbox abort timing was still too coarse because faults
  could only target the next matching segment
- milestone-based mailbox progress keeps tests honest without sleeps or
  custom device behavior

- add `deactivate/0` and `deactivate(:preop)` plus a public
  `:deactivated` state
- track the desired runtime target in the master and make
  activation-blocked and recovery flows settle to OP, SAFEOP, or PREOP
- add a dedicated deactivation helper and update docs and tests for the
  new lifecycle semantics

Rationale:
- separate leaving OP from tearing the session down so callers can keep
  a live session while outputs are off
- stop reconnect and recovery paths from auto-promoting slaves back to
  OP when the runtime was intentionally deactivated

feat(simulator): add command-targeted WKC skew

Summary:
- add `{:command_wkc_offset, command_name, delta}` as a queued or sticky
  simulator fault and route it through per-datagram WKC adjustment
- add simulator visibility for active command WKC offsets and cover the new
  fault with unit tests plus integration scenario 14
- refresh simulator docs and scenario notes so the command-targeted WKC path
  is part of the public fault model and scenario loop

Rationale:
- targeted logical WKC skew covered LRW contribution faults, but the simulator
  still could not model poll-specific or command-specific WKC degradation
- health-poll failures with a healthy LRW domain are a real class of master
  recovery path and need direct regression coverage
- the simulator docs should describe the currently supported fault shapes and
  keep the scenario backlog focused on the remaining mailbox timing gap

  logical datagram contributions
- add startup mailbox-abort coverage with a configured mailbox test driver
  plus new simulator scenarios 12 and 13
- refresh simulator docs and harden the integration harness so the standalone
  simulator suite is stable with the broader scenario set

Rationale:
- global WKC skew was too coarse to model one slave disappearing from the
  logical PDO contribution while fixed-address health polling stayed honest
- startup mailbox failures are a real PREOP activation path and needed direct
  simulator coverage, not just public SDO abort tests
- the simulator README and scenario notes should track the current fault API
  and scenario loop, and the test harness needed less aggressive defaults to
  avoid false startup flakes

feat(simulator): add milestone-aware fault scripts

Summary:
- replace the old exchange-only script wrapper with sequential
  `{:fault_script, steps}` handling in the simulator runtime
- allow scripts to pause on `{:wait_for_milestone, ...}` and resume into
  later exchange or slave-local fault steps
- add scenario 11 and update simulator docs and existing script-based
  tests to use the new abstraction

Rationale:
- `exchange_script` stopped matching the real API once scripts needed to
  mix waits and non-exchange faults
- moving to a single sequential script runner keeps fault timing inside
  the simulator instead of pushing brittle choreography into tests
- the extra snapshot visibility makes queued and waiting script state
  observable enough for integration assertions

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs test/integration/simulator/07_combined_fault_script_test.exs test/integration/simulator/08_delayed_slave_fault_script_test.exs test/integration/simulator/09_milestone_fault_script_test.exs test/integration/simulator/11_embedded_milestone_fault_script_test.exs

feat: align harness workflow with GitHub tooling (#807)

* feat(harness): align agent bootstrap with GitHub tooling

Summary:
- add a repo-root AGENTS.md entrypoint and remove the stale root agents.md
- replace the broken elixir bootstrap with a Python-focused worktree init flow
- move active workflow, command, and PR-body guidance to GitHub gh paths
- add regression coverage for docs sync, bootstrap dry-run, and PR body fetch

Rationale:
- origin/main still failed fresh-session bootstrap because the harness assumed an
  elixir subtree that no longer exists in this Python repository
- current unattended guidance still routed agents through stale Gitea tea paths,
  which conflicts with the available GitHub auth and the PR review feedback

- pytest tests/test_validate_docs_sync.py tests/test_worktree_init.py tests/test_validate_pr_body.py -q
- python3 scripts/validate_docs_sync.py
- bash .codex/worktree_init.sh --dry-run
- bash .codex/worktree_init.sh
- gh auth status && git ls-remote origin HEAD && gh pr status
- ruff check scripts tests

* fix(ci): tolerate missing rg in backtest gate

Summary:
- send backtest gate log output to stderr so auto mode selection stays clean
- fall back to grep when rg is unavailable in the runner environment
- add a regression test for auto mode without rg on PATH

Rationale:
- PR #807 surfaced a real CI-only failure where the backtest gate crashed
  before deciding skip/full because the hosted runner lacked rg
- the resulting log text leaked into selected_mode and produced an invalid
  BACKTEST_MODE failure even for docs-only changes

- pytest tests/test_backtest_gate.py tests/test_validate_docs_sync.py tests/test_worktree_init.py tests/test_validate_pr_body.py -q
- BACKTEST_MODE=auto BASE_REF=origin/main FORCE_FULL_BACKTEST=false bash scripts/backtest_gate.sh
- ruff check scripts tests

Summary:
- switch governance asset validation to .github PR template requirements
- require gh-based workflow and command tokens instead of tea_comment markers
- keep regression coverage green alongside the backtest-gate and harness tests

Rationale:
- PR #807 test workflow still enforced stale Gitea governance tokens after the
  docs were correctly moved to the GitHub harness path
- the validator should guard the active unattended workflow, not force the docs
  back toward deprecated tea-based instructions

Tests:

  fault}` and expose `scheduled_faults` in simulator snapshots
- add the new `08` delayed slave-fault integration scenario and update
  simulator docs to reflect the expanded fault API

  the PREOP mailbox abort scenario with a less brittle timeout budget

  relying on brittle sleeps or oversized exchange scripts

  timeouts during its initial PREOP roundtrip

Summary:
- move simulator authoring out of real slave drivers into optional
  `MyDriver.Simulator` adapters and rename `process_data_model` to
  `signal_model`

  `next_fault` and `pending_faults`
- add simulator scenario docs and integration coverage for timeout, WKC,
  disconnect, corruption, mailbox abort, safeop retreat, and combined
  scripts

Rationale:
- keep real drivers focused on runtime-facing identity and signal modeling
  instead of returning simulator-owned structs
- make simulator recovery scenarios deterministic without sticky
  inject-and-clear choreography in the tests
- document the simulator fault boundary so runtime faults and raw UDP
  corruption stay separate and spec-shaped

fix(harness): harden stateful gate preflights (#26)

* fix(harness): harden stateful gate preflights

Summary:
- add shared repo-sync and archived-plan validation helpers for
  publish, final-gate, and land decisions
- add a small CI status exporter plus land preflight and regression
  coverage for stale repo, plan, and gate inputs
- align workflow and standards docs with the enforced stateful gate
  contract and archive the harness plan for issues #9, #12, and #19

Rationale:
- prevent publish, final-gate, and land from making stateful decisions
  against stale refs, stale plan records, or mismatched machine input
- keep the repository workflow legible by making the gateable plan
  contract explicit in skills, standards, and archived evidence

Tests:
- git diff --check
- .agents/skills/loop-review-loop/scripts/review_regression.sh
- .agents/skills/loop-final-gate/scripts/stateful_gate_regression.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(harness): require clean state for gate evidence

Summary:
- require a clean working tree before exporting CI status, running
  final gate, or running land preflight
- update regression fixtures so temporary repos ignore `.local/` and
  cover dirty-worktree rejection paths
- refresh the archived harness plan with the post-rebase validation and
  review history on top of the newer main base

Rationale:
- keep gate evidence aligned with the exact published HEAD instead of
  allowing unpublished local edits to leak into decision artifacts
- preserve the archived plan as the authoritative execution record after
  the branch was rebased and recommitted

Tests:
- git diff --check
- .agents/skills/loop-review-loop/scripts/review_regression.sh
- .agents/skills/loop-final-gate/scripts/stateful_gate_regression.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(harness): enforce repo-sync prune preflights

Summary:
- add repo-sync preflight enforcement to review_init.sh
- extend review and stateful gate regressions to assert stale
  remote-tracking refs are pruned during sync
- refresh the archived harness plan with the latest review and
  validation evidence

Rationale:
- review-loop, publish, final-gate, and land should all fail on
  stale local remote state instead of making decisions from drifted refs
- the archived plan must stay aligned with the latest rebased branch
  evidence so publish and final gate do not rely on stale records

Tests:
- git diff --check
- bash -n .agents/skills/loop-review-loop/scripts/review_regression.sh
- bash -n .agents/skills/loop-final-gate/scripts/stateful_gate_regression.sh
- .agents/skills/loop-review-loop/scripts/review_regression.sh
- .agents/skills/loop-final-gate/scripts/stateful_gate_regression.sh
- .agents/skills/loop-review-loop/scripts/review_finalize.sh 20260311-151838 .local/loop/review-20260311-151838-*.json

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(harness): record publish outcome

Summary:
- record PR #26 and the published head SHA in the archived harness plan
- update the completion summary to reflect that publish has run
- keep the issue-linkage notes aligned with merge-time close behavior

Rationale:
- the archived plan should remain the source-of-truth record for
  publish/final-gate/land state after the PR is opened
- stale completion notes would otherwise contradict the actual branch
  lifecycle and weaken later gate evidence

Tests:
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(harness): stabilize publish evidence note

Summary:
- replace the self-stale published head SHA note with a stable PR metadata note
- keep the archived harness plan aligned with how publish evidence is actually sourced

Rationale:
- a tracked plan cannot safely embed its own latest commit SHA without
  immediately going stale on the next commit
- PR metadata remains the right source of truth for the current published
  head while the plan records the durable PR linkage

Tests:
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* ci(harness): add required-check workflow

Summary:
- add a minimal GitHub Actions workflow that runs the harness
  regression checks on pull requests to main
- document that final-gate requires at least one required GitHub
  status check on the protected base branch
- record the post-publish CI unblocker in the archived harness plan

Rationale:
- export_ci_status.sh correctly blocks when GitHub reports zero
  required checks, so this repository needed a minimal workflow to
  make final-gate reachable
- the workflow and standards updates keep the repository contract
  explicit instead of relying on tribal knowledge

Tests:
- git diff --check
- .agents/skills/loop-review-loop/scripts/review_regression.sh
- .agents/skills/loop-final-gate/scripts/stateful_gate_regression.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(ci): bootstrap harness workflow from branch pushes

Summary:
- trigger harness-checks on pushes to codex/* branches and main
- record the initial pull_request startup failure in the archived
  harness plan for traceability

Rationale:
- the new workflow needs a push-based bootstrap path before it exists
  on main, otherwise GitHub can mark the first pull_request run as a
  workflow startup failure with no jobs
- branch push runs still publish the check context needed to make the
  PR final-gate ready once branch protection marks it required

Tests:
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(ci): use shell checkout for local-only actions policy

Summary:
- replace actions/checkout with a pure-shell authenticated git fetch
  checkout in the harness-checks workflow
- record the repository local-only Actions policy in the archived
  harness plan as the reason for the startup failure

Rationale:
- this repository currently allows only local actions, so external
  actions like actions/checkout fail before any jobs start
- a shell checkout keeps the workflow compatible with current policy
  while still producing the check context final-gate needs

Tests:
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

- 删除已废弃生产脚本 scripts/run_api_prod.sh 与 scripts/run_webui_prod.sh，并同步 README 与运行时端口契约测试。
- 增加构造阶段 runtime bootstrap 与后台任务取消逻辑，降低 enable_webui 打开后的生命周期漏触发风险。

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Improve break-loop stack traces (#6257)

This reworks break-loop stack traces so the initial traceback and
later `Where()` output are driven by the same visible traceback
state.

The break loop now tracks both the real environment used for local
variable lookup and the first visible traceback frame. That makes
ordinary errors, kernel-thrown errors, and no-method-found errors
behave more consistently: `Where()` now shows the same top visible
frame that was shown on entry, while method-not-found still keeps
its helper environment available for `ShowArguments`,
`ShowDetails`, and `ShowMethods`.

This also adds `CurrentEnv()` for inspecting the currently
selected break-loop environment and updates the break-loop docs
and tests accordingly.

On top of that, visible stack frames are now rendered with
numbered prefixes in the style `*[1]`, ` [2]`, ` [3]`, where the
`*` denotes the active frame. For me, that makes it *much* easier
to use `DownEnv`/`UpEnv` correctly, and also increases readability
of in longer traces. The manual examples and special REPL
transcripts were refreshed to match the new output.

AI disclosure: prepared with help from Codex for implementation,
tests, and documentation.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(postgres): document data-copy migration workflow

Summary:
- add explicit guidance for drafting production migration SQL from
  dev or local Postgres rows
- document checks for source data, target defaults, and foreign-key
  handling before writing copied data into a pending migration
- clarify that generated primary keys should not be copied directly
  and dependent inserts should resolve IDs in SQL

Rationale:
- make the Postgres skill safer when users want to promote selected
  dev data into production-facing migration files
- capture the expected adaptation work so copied values are treated
  as a draft instead of assumed production-ready data

Tests:
- not run (docs-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
